title: ws.rs GROUP_ADD/SEND/LEAVE: group_id not length-validated before DB ops
description: Trigger: authenticated client sends GROUP_ADD (or GROUP_SEND, GROUP_LEAVE) with a group_id up to ~1 MiB (within the WS frame cap). Bad behavior: the full string is allocated and passed directly to get_group(), add_group_member_capped(), is_group_member(), and list_group_members() DB calls with no length check. The member_pub_id field on GROUP_ADD is validated to exactly 64 hex chars, but group_id has no corresponding guard. At 60 req/min rate limit this enables sustained allocation of ~1 MiB per request. Fix: add a length guard (e.g. ≤128 bytes) on params.group_id before the first DB call in all three handlers, consistent with the pattern used for pub_id fields.
